articles.title: MuddyWater Uses DLL Side-Loading in Espionage Campaign Targeting 9 Countries
articles.summary: MuddyWater, an Iranian hacking group, launched a widespread espionage campaign in early 2026 targeting at least nine organizations across multiple countries using DLL side-loading techniques. The attack affected organizations in critical sectors including manufacturing, education, government, finance, and professional services across four continents. This campaign demonstrates the group's continued focus on stealing sensitive information from high-value targets rather than deploying ransomware.
articles.whyItMatters: DLL side-loading is a sophisticated attack technique that exploits legitimate processes to avoid detection, making it harder for traditional security tools to identify the threat. Organizations in critical infrastructure and government sectors need to understand this specific attack vector to implement proper defensive measures and monitor for suspicious DLL loading behavior.

articles.title: [THN Webinar] New AI DDoS Attacks Are Smarter. Learn How to Fight Back
articles.summary: Cybercriminals are increasingly leveraging artificial intelligence to enhance their attack capabilities, making DDoS attacks more sophisticated and difficult to defend against. AI tools enable attackers to identify system vulnerabilities faster and automate the discovery of weaknesses at scale. This shift represents a significant escalation in the threat landscape as attackers move beyond manual methods to AI-powered assault techniques.
articles.whyItMatters: Understanding how adversaries weaponize AI is critical for security teams to adapt their defense strategies and detection mechanisms. As attacks become more automated and intelligent, traditional static defenses become increasingly ineffective, making this a pressing concern for organizations of all sizes.

articles.title: Microsoft Patches SharePoint RCE Flaw CVE-2026-45659 Across Server Versions
articles.summary: Microsoft released security updates to fix CVE-2026-45659, a remote code execution vulnerability in SharePoint that requires no special conditions to exploit. The flaw, which involves unsafe deserialization of untrusted data, has been rated with a CVSS score of 8.8 and marked as important severity. Organizations running affected SharePoint server versions need to apply these patches promptly to prevent potential attacks.
articles.whyItMatters: This vulnerability poses a significant risk because it allows attackers to execute arbitrary code on SharePoint servers without any special requirements, making it an attractive target for widespread attacks. Organizations that delay patching expose their infrastructure to potential compromise, data theft, or system takeover. Prompt patching is critical given SharePoint's widespread use in enterpr

articles.title: MFA Prompt Bombing: Why Your Second Factor Isn't Saving You
articles.summary: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is being undermined by a new attack technique called MFA prompt bombing, where attackers trick users into approving login requests rather than stealing the second factor itself. This method exploits human behavior and social engineering to bypass what many organizations consider their strongest security defense. The attack demonstrates that even strong technical controls can fail when users are manipulated into voluntarily compromising them.
articles.whyItMatters: Organizations relying on MFA as their primary security control may have a false sense of protection if users can be socially engineered into approving unauthorized login attempts. This threat requires immediate user training and implementation of additional safeguards like location-based verification or challenge-response mechanisms alongside standard MFA.

articles.title: CERT-In Recommends 12-Hour Patching for Internet-Facing Flaws Amid AI-Assisted Attacks
articles.summary: India's CERT-In has released new security guidelines requiring organizations to patch critical vulnerabilities in internet-facing systems within 12 hours when possible. The mandate responds to increasing threats from cybercriminals using artificial intelligence and language models to automate and accelerate vulnerability exploitation. These stricter timelines aim to reduce the window of opportunity for attackers to compromise exposed systems.
articles.whyItMatters: Organizations must now drastically compress their patch cycles from traditional timelines to 12 hours for critical internet-exposed vulnerabilities, requiring significant operational changes and automation investments. The advisory highlights how AI-powered exploitation is accelerating threat actor capabilities, making rapid patching essential to prevent large-scale breaches. This guidance sets a

articles.title: Charter confirms data breach after ShinyHunters extortion threat
articles.summary: Charter Communications, a major U.S. telecommunications provider, confirmed that it experienced a data breach after the ShinyHunters extortion group threatened to release the stolen information unless a ransom was paid. This incident highlights how large infrastructure companies remain attractive targets for cybercriminals using extortion tactics. The breach exposes the personal information of potentially millions of customers who rely on Charter for internet, TV, and phone services.
articles.whyItMatters: Breaches at major telecommunications providers directly affect millions of customers and can compromise sensitive personal information used for identity theft and fraud. This case demonstrates how extortion-based attacks on critical infrastructure continue to escalate, setting a concerning precedent for other large service providers. Understanding these threats helps individuals and organizations

articles.title: How Varonis Atlas integrates Claude Compliance API for AI governance
articles.summary: Varonis Atlas is integrating Anthropic's Claude Compliance API to enhance AI governance within enterprise environments. The integration enables organizations to monitor how AI tools access and interact with sensitive business data, helping them investigate potential risks and maintain compliance standards. This approach provides better visibility into AI tool usage patterns across the organization.
articles.whyItMatters: As AI tools become increasingly embedded in enterprise workflows, understanding how they access sensitive data is critical for risk management and regulatory compliance. This integration addresses a growing security gap by providing visibility into AI-data interactions, helping organizations prevent unauthorized access and maintain compliance with data protection regulations.

articles.title: Microsoft Defender can now automatically isolate hacked endpoints
articles.summary: Microsoft is developing a new feature for Defender for Endpoint that will automatically isolate compromised devices from the network when they are detected as hacked. This automatic isolation stops attackers from using an infected computer to spread laterally and attack other systems on the same network. The capability is currently in testing and represents an improvement in how organizations can respond quickly to security threats.
articles.whyItMatters: Automatic isolation of compromised endpoints significantly reduces the time attackers have to move through a network and infect other systems, which is a critical phase in most major cyber attacks. This feature can help organizations minimize damage and contain breaches faster than manual intervention alone. Implementing automated response mechanisms like this strengthens overall network defense a

articles.title: Webinar: Too many tools are slowing network incident response
articles.summary: During network incidents, IT teams waste valuable time switching between multiple separate tools like monitoring dashboards, ticketing systems, and communication platforms. This webinar discusses how automation and AI-powered workflows can streamline incident response by reducing manual handoffs and coordination overhead. By consolidating tools and automating repetitive tasks, organizations can respond to security incidents faster and more effectively.
articles.whyItMatters: Tool fragmentation directly slows incident response times, which is critical because every minute of delay increases damage potential during active security incidents. Implementing automation and AI-assisted workflows can significantly reduce mean time to respond (MTTR), helping organizations contain threats before they escalate into major breaches or widespread compromise.

articles.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Drupal vulnerability
articles.summary: The U.S. government agency CISA has mandated that federal agencies patch a critical SQL injection vulnerability in Drupal, a widely-used content management system, by Wednesday evening. This vulnerability is currently being actively exploited by attackers in the wild. The urgent deadline reflects the serious and immediate threat this flaw poses to government systems and infrastructure.
articles.whyItMatters: Active exploitation combined with a federal mandate indicates this vulnerability poses an immediate, widespread risk to critical infrastructure and government operations. SQL injection vulnerabilities can allow attackers to steal, modify, or delete sensitive data, making this a priority threat that organizations must address urgently to prevent compromise.

articles.title: KnowledgeDeliver LMS Flaw Exploited to Deploy Godzilla and Cobalt Strike
articles.summary: A security flaw in Digital Knowledge's KnowledgeDeliver learning management system was exploited to install dangerous malware on affected systems. Attackers used hard-coded security keys in the software to deploy a Godzilla web shell and then install Cobalt Strike, a tool commonly used for unauthorized network access. The vulnerability (CVE-2026-5426) has since been patched, but it demonstrates how educational institutions using this software were at risk.
articles.whyItMatters: This attack shows how flaws in educational technology can be weaponized to gain deep access into institutional networks. Organizations using KnowledgeDeliver needed to patch immediately to prevent unauthorized access and data theft. The use of hard-coded security keys is a common but critical mistake that can expose multiple organizations simultaneously.

articles.title: npm Adds 2FA-Gated Publishing and Package Install Controls Against Supply Chain Attacks
articles.summary: GitHub has introduced staged publishing for npm packages, a security feature that requires maintainers to complete a two-factor authentication (2FA) verification before releasing packages to the public. This new control helps prevent unauthorized or malicious package releases by adding a mandatory human approval step in the publishing process. The feature addresses supply chain security risks by ensuring that only verified maintainers can deploy code to npm's repository.
articles.whyItMatters: Supply chain attacks through compromised or malicious package releases remain a significant threat to software security. By requiring 2FA-gated approval for npm package publishing, this feature substantially reduces the risk of unauthorized releases that could distribute malware or vulnerable code to millions of developers. This represents an important industry shift toward making software reposit

articles.title: Packagist Supply Chain Attack Infects 8 Packages Using GitHub-Hosted Linux Malware
articles.summary: Attackers compromised eight packages on Packagist, a popular PHP package repository, by injecting malicious code that downloads and executes a Linux binary from GitHub. Rather than modifying the expected composer.json file, the attackers inserted code into package.json to target JavaScript projects, demonstrating a sophisticated cross-platform attack strategy. This coordinated campaign shows how attackers can exploit trusted package repositories to distribute malware to thousands of developers.
articles.whyItMatters: Supply chain attacks through package repositories like Packagist pose an enormous risk because developers automatically pull updates into production environments, potentially infecting thousands of downstream projects at scale. The use of multiple file types (composer.json and package.json) demonstrates attackers are becoming more sophisticated in evading detection and targeting diverse ecosystems

articles.title: Claude Mythos AI Finds 10,000 High-Severity Flaws in Widely Used Software
articles.summary: Anthropic's Project Glasswing, a new AI-powered security initiative, has identified over 10,000 high and critical vulnerabilities in essential software used worldwide since its launch last month. This defensive project demonstrates how artificial intelligence can rapidly discover security flaws in systemically important software that many organizations depend on. The findings highlight the scale of hidden vulnerabilities in widely deployed systems that could pose significant risks if left unpatched.
articles.whyItMatters: This discovery reveals that critical infrastructure and widely-used software contain thousands of serious vulnerabilities that pose immediate risks to organizations globally. The scale of these findings underscores the urgent need for rapid patching and demonstrates AI's growing role in proactive threat detection before malicious actors can exploit these flaws.

articles.title: Laravel-Lang PHP Packages Compromised to Deliver Cross-Platform Credential Stealer
articles.summary: Attackers compromised multiple PHP packages under Laravel-Lang, a popular open-source project, to distribute malware that steals credentials across different platforms. Four affected packages (lang, http-statuses, attributes, and actions) were manipulated through a supply chain attack. This campaign highlights how threat actors can exploit trusted software dependencies to reach thousands of developers and their applications.
articles.whyItMatters: Supply chain attacks are particularly dangerous because compromised packages can be automatically pulled into thousands of projects, giving attackers widespread access to credentials and sensitive data. Developers who relied on these popular Laravel-Lang packages may have unknowingly integrated malware into their applications, requiring immediate investigation and remediation across affected syste

articles.title: Netherlands seizes 800 servers of hosting firm enabling cyberattacks
articles.summary: Dutch financial crime investigators arrested two men and seized 800 servers from a web hosting company that was being used to facilitate cyberattacks, interference operations, and disinformation campaigns. This action demonstrates law enforcement's growing ability to identify and shut down infrastructure that criminals rely on to conduct large-scale cyber operations. The seizure removes a significant platform that was enabling multiple types of malicious activities.
articles.whyItMatters: This case highlights how hosting providers can be weaponized to enable widespread cyber threats, and shows that international law enforcement is increasingly targeting the underlying infrastructure that supports attackers rather than just individual malware or campaigns. Organizations should audit their hosting and cloud providers for security practices, as compromised infrastructure can expose th

articles.title: Former US execs plead guilty to aiding tech support scammers
articles.summary: Two former executives from a call-tracking company pleaded guilty to helping operate a tech support scam that defrauded people worldwide over many years. The executives deliberately hid evidence of the fraud scheme, which targeted unsuspecting victims seeking technical help. This case highlights how corporate insiders can enable large-scale scam operations.
articles.whyItMatters: This case demonstrates how tech support scams operate with corporate infrastructure and insider cooperation, affecting victims globally. Understanding that legitimate companies can be exploited by their own executives helps organizations implement better internal controls and compliance monitoring. The guilty plea reinforces that law enforcement actively pursues those who knowingly enable fraud sc

articles.title: Trend Micro warns of Apex One zero-day exploited in the wild
articles.summary: Trend Micro has discovered and is addressing a zero-day vulnerability in its Apex One security software that attackers are actively exploiting in real-world attacks against Windows computers. This flaw represents a significant concern because the company's own endpoint protection tool—designed to defend systems—has become a target for threat actors. Users of Apex One need to apply security patches urgently to prevent attackers from compromising their protected systems.
articles.whyItMatters: When security software itself contains exploitable vulnerabilities that are actively being attacked, it creates a critical trust and protection gap for organizations relying on that tool. This situation demands immediate patching across affected deployments, as attackers can potentially bypass the endpoint protection that enterprises depend on to defend their networks.

articles.title: Drupal: Critical SQL injection flaw now targeted in attacks
articles.summary: Drupal has alerted users about a highly critical SQL injection vulnerability that is currently being exploited by attackers in active campaigns. This flaw, which was recently disclosed, allows hackers to potentially access or manipulate database information on vulnerable Drupal sites. Organizations running Drupal need to apply security patches immediately to prevent unauthorized access to their systems.
articles.whyItMatters: SQL injection vulnerabilities in widely-used platforms like Drupal pose an immediate threat because attackers are already leveraging this flaw in real-world attacks. Unpatched Drupal installations could allow attackers to steal sensitive data, compromise user accounts, or take control of websites, making rapid patching essential for organizations relying on this content management system.

articles.title: Why Chargebacks are Just One Piece of the Fraud Puzzle
articles.summary: Fraud losses extend far beyond chargebacks, including false declines, account takeovers, and customer abuse that collectively damage both revenue and user trust. Security teams need comprehensive visibility across multiple fraud vectors rather than focusing on a single metric like chargebacks. IPQS highlights how understanding the full scope of fraud-related risks helps organizations better protect customers and their bottom line.
articles.whyItMatters: Organizations often underestimate total fraud losses by only tracking chargebacks, missing significant revenue leakage from false declines and account compromises. Comprehensive fraud visibility enables better risk assessment and customer protection strategies, directly impacting financial performance and reputation. Understanding the full fraud landscape helps security teams allocate resources mo

articles.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Linux Flaws, Defender 0-Days, Router Botnets, and Supply Chain Chaos
articles.summary: This week's cybersecurity landscape saw multiple critical threats emerge, including vulnerabilities in Linux systems, zero-day exploits affecting Windows Defender, and botnet attacks on routers. Companies discovered they had neglected to patch systems for years, while phishing attacks became more sophisticated and harder to detect. Supply chain compromises added another layer of risk as malicious code infiltrated development tools.
articles.whyItMatters: This recap highlights the convergence of multiple attack vectors—unpatched legacy systems, zero-days in security tools, and compromised development environments—that create compounding risks for organizations. The trend of phishing becoming more targeted and the discovery of years-old unpatched systems demonstrates that both cyber attackers and defenders are racing against organizational complacen

articles.title: Ghost CMS CVE-2026-26980 Exploited to Hijack 700+ Sites for ClickFix Attacks
articles.summary: Attackers are actively exploiting a critical SQL injection flaw (CVE-2026-26980) in Ghost CMS to compromise over 700 websites. The vulnerability allows unauthenticated users to extract sensitive data and inject malicious JavaScript code. These compromised sites are then used to launch ClickFix attacks, a social engineering technique that tricks users into performing harmful actions.
articles.whyItMatters: With over 700 sites already compromised and active exploitation ongoing, this represents an immediate threat to website owners and users. The combination of unauthenticated access, widespread vulnerable deployments, and secondary attack chains (ClickFix) demonstrates how a single critical vulnerability can cascade into large-scale harm across the internet.

articles.title: The Alert Firehose Finally Meets Its Match
articles.summary: Network Detection and Response (NDR) systems have historically been criticized for generating excessive alerts and noise, making them difficult for security teams to use effectively. Modern NDR solutions now incorporate artificial intelligence capabilities that enable faster threat detection, more efficient alert triage, and significantly fewer false positives. This evolution suggests that NDR technology is finally becoming a practical and valuable tool for cybersecurity professionals rather than a source of overwhelming data.
articles.whyItMatters: AI-enhanced NDR tools directly address a critical pain point in security operations—alert fatigue and false positives—which enables security teams to respond more effectively to genuine threats. Organizations investing in modern NDR solutions with AI capabilities can improve their threat hunting capabilities and reduce mean time to detection (MTTD), ultimately strengthening their defensive posture

articles.title: Lazarus Deploys RemotePE Memory-Only RAT Against Financial and Crypto Firms
articles.summary: The Lazarus Group, a North Korean-linked hacking organization, has been using a sophisticated malware called RemotePE to attack financial and cryptocurrency companies. RemotePE operates in memory only, making it harder to detect, and is deployed through a multi-stage attack chain involving specialized loaders like DPAPILoader and RemotePELoader. This cross-platform threat demonstrates how advanced threat actors target high-value sectors with stealthy, hard-to-detect techniques.
articles.whyItMatters: Financial and crypto organizations are prime targets for nation-state actors seeking to steal funds or disrupt operations, and memory-only malware like RemotePE evades traditional detection methods, making this threat particularly dangerous. Understanding how Lazarus deploys this malware helps security teams recognize similar attack patterns and implement stronger defenses against sophisticated ad

articles.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
articles.summary: Attackers launched a coordinated campaign called TrapDoor that distributed credential-stealing malware through 34 malicious packages across popular package managers: npm (JavaScript), PyPI (Python), and Crates.io (Rust). The attack began on May 22, 2026, and spread across hundreds of package versions in waves, targeting developers who unknowingly downloaded infected code. This multi-ecosystem attack highlights how supply chain threats can compromise multiple programming communities simultaneously.
articles.whyItMatters: This attack is critical because it compromises trusted package repositories that millions of developers rely on daily, potentially affecting countless downstream applications and organizations. The cross-ecosystem nature means attackers infiltrated multiple major programming communities simultaneously, increasing the attack surface exponentially. Developers who used these packages without verifica

articles.title: Anthropic’s restricted Claude Mythos model may be coming to Claude Code
articles.summary: Anthropic is preparing to publicly release its Mythos model through Claude Code, a restricted AI model that was previously announced in April. The model has been flagged as potentially posing significant security risks to both private and public software systems. This rollout could expose vulnerabilities if proper safeguards are not implemented.
articles.whyItMatters: The public rollout of a restricted AI model with known security risks requires careful consideration of how malicious actors could exploit it to attack software systems. Organizations using Claude Code need to understand these risks and implement appropriate security measures to protect their applications and data. This highlights the growing challenge of securing powerful AI tools in production e

articles.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing service targeting Microsoft 365 accounts
articles.summary: The FBI has issued a warning about Kali365, a phishing service that criminals use to steal Microsoft 365 accounts. This service exploits OAuth device code authentication to bypass multi-factor authentication and capture session tokens, giving attackers unauthorized access to business email and cloud services.
articles.whyItMatters: This threat is critical because it targets widely-used Microsoft 365 accounts and can bypass MFA protections that organizations rely on for security. Attackers can gain persistent access to corporate email and sensitive cloud data, making this a serious risk for businesses of all sizes.

articles.title: Ghost CMS SQL injection flaw exploited in large-scale ClickFix campaign
articles.summary: Attackers are actively exploiting a critical SQL injection vulnerability in Ghost CMS to inject malicious code that initiates ClickFix social engineering attacks. This large-scale campaign demonstrates how vulnerabilities in popular content management systems can be weaponized to compromise multiple websites simultaneously. Users of Ghost CMS are at immediate risk of having their sites hijacked to distribute malware or conduct phishing attacks.
articles.whyItMatters: This vulnerability poses an immediate threat because it is actively being exploited in the wild at scale, affecting a widely-used CMS platform. Organizations running Ghost CMS need to patch urgently to prevent their sites from being compromised and used as attack vectors. The combination of SQL injection and ClickFix attacks creates a multi-stage threat that can lead to system compromise and socia

articles.title: Laravel Lang packages hijacked to deploy credential-stealing malware
articles.summary: Attackers compromised the Laravel Lang localization packages on GitHub and used manipulated version tags to distribute malicious code through Composer, a popular PHP package manager. Developers who installed these tainted packages unknowingly exposed their systems to credential-stealing malware. This supply chain attack demonstrates how exploiting trusted software repositories can put thousands of developers at risk simultaneously.
articles.whyItMatters: This attack highlights a critical vulnerability in the software supply chain where attackers target widely-used open-source packages to distribute malware at scale. Developers relying on automatic dependency updates or package managers may have unknowingly installed compromised code, making this a widespread threat affecting numerous applications. Organizations must implement stricter dependency v

articles.title: Italy disrupts CINEMAGOAL piracy app that stole streaming auth codes
articles.summary: Italian law enforcement shut down the CINEMAGOAL piracy application, which was illegally distributing access to popular streaming services like Netflix, Disney+, and Spotify by stealing authentication credentials. The operation dismantled a significant piracy network that compromised user accounts across multiple platforms. This takedown highlights how criminals exploit stolen login information to distribute unauthorized access at scale.
articles.whyItMatters: This case demonstrates the real-world consequences of weak password practices and account credential theft, reminding users that stolen login information can be weaponized for large-scale piracy operations. It also shows that law enforcement agencies are actively targeting organized cybercrime ecosystems, which may deter future similar ventures but underscores the ongoing risk of account compromis

articles.title: Critical Zero-Day Actively Exploited in Ivanti Connect Secure VPN Appliances
articles.summary: A critical zero-day vulnerability has been discovered in Ivanti Connect Secure VPN appliances, allowing unauthenticated remote code execution. Threat actors believed to be state-sponsored are actively exploiting this flaw to establish persistence inside corporate networks. Ivanti has released an emergency patch and strongly recommends all customers apply it immediately alongside a full factory reset of affected devices.
articles.whyItMatters: VPN appliances are the front door to corporate networks. An unauthenticated RCE flaw means attackers can fully compromise an organisation without valid credentials. Because exploitation is active and attributed to nation-state actors, any unpatched device should be treated as already breached.

articles.title: Apache OFBiz RCE Flaw Added to CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalogue
articles.summary: A critical remote code execution vulnerability in Apache OFBiz (CVSS 9.8) has been confirmed as actively exploited and added to the CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalogue. The flaw requires no authentication and could allow a complete server takeover. Organisations running OFBiz are urged to upgrade to the latest patched release immediately.
articles.whyItMatters: ERP systems hold the most sensitive business data — financials, HR records, and customer information. A CVSS 9.8 unauthenticated RCE flaw on a business-critical platform is as serious as vulnerabilities get. CISA's KEV listing means federal agencies must patch within days, and all organisations should treat it equally urgently.

articles.title: Medusa Ransomware Group Disrupts Services Across Multiple NHS Trusts in England
articles.summary: Several NHS Trusts in England were hit by a coordinated ransomware attack attributed to the Medusa group, disrupting patient appointment systems, internal communications, and clinical records access. The attackers claim to have exfiltrated over 1.5 TB of patient and staff data. NHS England has activated its major incident response protocol and is working with the NCSC to contain the breach.
articles.whyItMatters: Ransomware attacks on healthcare are life-threatening, not just financially damaging. Disrupted appointment systems and inaccessible patient records can delay critical care and put lives at risk. Exfiltrated patient data also triggers serious UK GDPR obligations and could result in significant regulatory fines.

articles.title: 50 Million Customer Records Exposed in Retailer Breach Traced to Third-Party API
articles.summary: A major European retailer disclosed a data breach affecting approximately 50 million customers after attackers exploited an unsecured third-party API endpoint. Exposed data includes names, email addresses, physical addresses, and hashed passwords. The breach went undetected for over three months, highlighting the risks of poor third-party security monitoring.
articles.whyItMatters: With 50 million records exposed and a three-month detection gap, this breach illustrates how third-party API risk is frequently underestimated in supply chain security assessments. Under GDPR, the retailer faces fines of up to 4% of global annual turnover, and affected customers are at elevated risk of phishing and credential stuffing attacks.

articles.title: LummaC3 Infostealer Uses Kernel-Level Evasion to Bypass Leading EDR Platforms
articles.summary: Researchers have identified LummaC3, a new infostealer variant that abuses legitimate kernel drivers to disable endpoint detection and response tools before executing its payload. The malware spreads through malvertising campaigns disguised as popular software installers. Once active, it silently exfiltrates browser credentials, session cookies, and cryptocurrency wallet data.
articles.whyItMatters: EDR bypass capabilities significantly raise the cost of defence. Stolen session cookies allow attackers to hijack authenticated sessions and bypass MFA entirely, meaning victims can be compromised even with strong passwords and two-factor authentication. The malvertising delivery method also means any user searching for software downloads could encounter it.

articles.title: Quishing Surge: QR Code Phishing Campaigns Targeting Corporate Microsoft 365 Users
articles.summary: Researchers have documented a significant increase in QR code phishing attacks, known as quishing, targeting enterprise Microsoft 365 users. Attackers embed malicious QR codes into PDF attachments sent from convincing corporate-looking senders. When scanned on a personal device, the codes redirect users to fake Microsoft login pages that capture credentials and MFA tokens in real time.
articles.whyItMatters: QR codes are much harder for email security gateways to analyse than traditional URLs, giving attackers an effective evasion method. The technique is particularly dangerous because employees often scan QR codes on personal phones with fewer corporate security controls, and real-time phishing proxies can capture MFA tokens before users notice anything is wrong.

articles.title: Misconfigured AWS S3 Bucket Exposes 200,000 Healthcare Patient Records
articles.summary: A publicly accessible Amazon Web Services S3 bucket belonging to a US healthcare provider was discovered by a security researcher, exposing over 200,000 patient records including names, dates of birth, diagnoses, and insurance details. The bucket has since been secured, but it is unknown how long the data was exposed or whether malicious actors accessed it before disclosure.
articles.whyItMatters: Cloud misconfigurations remain one of the most common and avoidable causes of data breaches. Healthcare records are among the most sensitive and regulated data types, with HIPAA violations carrying significant penalties. This incident is a reminder that cloud storage permissions must be reviewed regularly using CSPM tools, not just at initial deployment.

articles.title: NCSC Publishes Updated MFA Guidance, Recommends Phishing-Resistant Authentication Methods
articles.summary: The UK National Cyber Security Centre has released updated multi-factor authentication guidance for organisations, emphasising phishing-resistant methods such as hardware security keys (FIDO2/WebAuthn) and passkeys over SMS-based one-time passwords. SMS OTPs remain vulnerable to SIM-swapping, SS7 interception, and real-time phishing proxy attacks.
articles.whyItMatters: MFA is one of the most effective controls against account takeover, but not all MFA methods offer equal protection. Many organisations still rely on SMS OTPs that modern attacks can bypass. This NCSC guidance helps security teams choose authentication methods that genuinely resist current attack techniques like real-time phishing and SIM-swapping.
